title: doratoxylon littorale
intro: Doratoxylon littorale, also known as the Philippine ironwood or Pacific ironwood, is a species of tree in the family Euphorbiaceae. This plant has been utilized in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Doratoxylon littorale include reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of Doratoxylon littorale include extracts, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, ointments, and salves for various health applications.
